metadata.disclaimer: Alpha research prototype. AI-generated analysis with human advisory input. Data and model have not been independently verified. Do not use for policy, legal, or administrative decisions. The Lanka Data Foundation accepts no responsibility for decisions based on this analysis.

dimensions.name: Mandate Completeness (MCS)
dimensions.description: How fully an act defines its institutional mandate: bodies established, powers granted, governance depth, functional breadth, and cross-linkage.

dimensions.name: Institutional Vitality (IVS)
dimensions.description: Per-body assessment of meeting clarity, quorum rules, reporting obligations, dissent protection, and leadership structure.

dimensions.name: Decision Governance Quality (DGQ)
dimensions.description: Per-body evaluation of composition diversity, member sufficiency, power breadth, and accountability chain.

dimensions.name: Power Dormancy Index (PDI)
dimensions.description: Penalty applied when bodies have unknown or superseded operational status, indicating potential legislative deadwood.

dimensions.name: Data Confidence Modifier (DCM)
dimensions.description: Scaling factor (0.4-1.0) based on confidence levels in legislative framework, historical details, and current operational status data.

bodyWeights.description: Top-tier councils and authorities that set policy direction (e.g., Medical Council, NMRA, NATA).
Bodies providing expert advisory input (e.g., Food Advisory Committee, Nursing Homes Advisory Board).
Boards managing institutions, foundations, and departments (e.g., SJGH Board, Suwaseriya Foundation).
Sub-committees and evaluation committees handling specific functions (e.g., MEC, MDEC, Appeals Committee).

gradingScale.meaning: Strong governance compliance — mandate is well-defined and institutional structures are active.
Good compliance — most governance structures in place with minor gaps.
Moderate compliance — functional mandate exists but institutional vitality is mixed.
Weak compliance — significant gaps in institutional operationalization.
Failing compliance — mandate exists largely on paper, institutions dormant or superseded.

bodies.name: Health Council

reportingMandated.source: Secretary to the Ministry after each meeting

dissentProtected.source: Members can record written dissent with reasons

compositionDiversity.source: Director of Health Services (non-voting) + member mix

pdiBreakdown.reason: Health Council has mandated meetings but operational status is unknown

powers: Make recommendations for improvement of health services
Tender advice to Minister on referred matters

bodies.name: Regional Hospitals Board

compositionDiversity.source: Unknown + member mix

pdiBreakdown.reason: Regional Hospitals Board has been superseded but parent act not repealed

powers: Exercise Hospital Committee functions (Section 17)
Manage regional hospital operations

bodies.name: Hospital Committee

compositionDiversity.source: Nominated by Minister (can vote) + member mix

pdiBreakdown.reason: Hospital Committee has been superseded but parent act not repealed

powers: Manage individual hospital operations (Section 12)
Report annually on hospital activities (Section 14)

bodies.name: College Council

compositionDiversity.source: Director-General of Health Services + member mix

powers: Manage and superintend the Ceylon Medical College (Section 8)
Make rules for the management of the College (Section 10)
Superintend examinations conducted at the College (Section 9)
Control admission of students (Section 8)
Manage college property and finances (Section 11)

bodies.name: Sri Lanka Medical Council

compositionDiversity.source: President of the Council + member mix

powers: Maintain the Medical Register and Dental Register (Part IV, Sections 23-34A)
Grant provisional and full registration to medical practitioners (Part V)
Grant registration to dentists (Part VI)
Register midwives (Part VII), pharmacists (Part VIII), para-medical assistants (Part VIIIA)
Register nurses (Part IX — repealed by Sri Lanka Nurses' Council Act, No. 19 of 1988)
Register professions supplementary to medicine (Part VIII AA)
Conduct licensing examinations (Section 22B)
Recognize foreign medical and dental qualifications (Section 22C)
Inquire into professional misconduct and remove practitioners from register (Section 22D-22F)
Make regulations for registration and practice standards (Section 22M)
Approve medical curricula and training programs

bodies.name: Medical Wants Committee

compositionDiversity.source: Unknown + member mix

powers: Advise Minister on hospital and dispensary construction needs (Section 26(a))
Review annual statement and estimates under Section 30 (Section 26(b))
Grant rebates on export duties to proprietors who provide adequate medical care (Section 27)

dcmBreakdown.rule: Two or more low confidence levels → 0.4

bodies.name: Board of Trustees

meetingClarity.source: Not specified — Board sets own rules (S.3(10))

chairType.source: Secretary of the Ministry of the Minister
chairType.label: Ex-officio

compositionDiversity.source: Secretary of the Ministry of the Minister + member mix

pdiBreakdown.reason: Board of Trustees has mandated meetings but operational status is unknown

powers: Acquire, hold, manage, sell, and mortgage property (S.5)
Borrow from banks/lending institutions with Minister's concurrence, up to aggregate of fixed deposits (S.5(bb), added by Amendment No. 17 of 1984)
Invest moneys subject to Minister's direction (S.8)
Appoint officers and servants, determine remuneration from Fund income (S.9)
Perform all acts necessary to give effect to the Act (S.5)

bodies.name: Nursing Homes Advisory Board

compositionDiversity.source: Director-General of Teaching Hospitals + member mix

powers: Advise the Minister upon matters affecting the administration of the Act (S.9(1))

bodies.name: Private Health Services Regulatory Council (PHSRC)

meetingClarity.source: Not specified in Act — Council sets own procedure

compositionDiversity.source: Director-General of Health Services + member mix

powers: Register private medical institutions through Provincial Directors (S.3)

Grade registered institutions based on standards (S.10(d))

Inspect private medical institutions without prior notice, at any time by day or night (S.14)
Prosecute offences under the Act (S.15-16)
Delegate duties and functions to members or committees (S.11)
Make rules on infrastructure, staffing, and disposal standards (S.19)

dissentProtected.source: Majority voting; presiding officer has casting vote (S.9)

chairType.source: President (elected from among elected members; S.7)

compositionDiversity.source: President (elected from among elected members; S.7) + member mix

powers: Register qualified nurses and maintain the nursing register (S.11(a), S.16)
Charge and collect registration fees (S.11(b))
Suspend, remove, or restore nurses from the register (S.11(c), S.20)
Recommend conditions of recruitment for nurses (S.11(d))
Recommend curricula for nursing education (S.11(e))
Recommend textbooks for nursing training (S.11(f))
Ensure welfare of nurses (S.11(g))
Inspect nursing education institutions (S.11(h))
Prescribe code of ethics for the nursing profession (S.11(i))
Appoint officers and staff (S.11(j))
Delegate powers to committees of at least 5 members (S.12)

acts.title: Sri Jayewardenepura General Hospital Board Act

compositionDiversity.source: Assessed from act text

powers: Receive grants, subsidies, donations, gifts, and bequests (S.7(a))
Hold, purchase, take on lease, or otherwise acquire movable and immovable property (S.7(b))
Sell, mortgage, lease, exchange, or otherwise dispose of any property (S.7(c))
Appoint, dismiss, and exercise disciplinary control over employees (S.7(d))
Administer the Fund established under S.10 (S.7(e))
Open and maintain bank accounts as approved by the Minister (S.7(f))
Borrow money with the Minister's approval (S.7(g))
Enter into contracts (S.7(h))
Do all things necessary or expedient for the proper management and administration of the Hospital (S.7(i))

bodies.name: Committee of Management

compositionDiversity.source: Assessed from act text

powers: Day-to-day administration of the Hospital as delegated by the Board (S.8)

acts.title: National Authority on Tobacco and Alcohol Act

bodies.name: National Authority on Tobacco and Alcohol (NATA)

meetingClarity.source: Not specified in Act — Authority sets own procedure (S.9)

reportingMandated.source: Annual report including audited accounts (S.29); annual audit under S.23

compositionDiversity.source: Chairman appointed by the Minister (S.8) + member mix

powers: Advise the Government on national policy for elimination of tobacco and alcohol-related harm (S.14(a))
Make recommendations on tax and pricing measures for tobacco and alcohol products (S.14(b))
Recommend measures to restrict or prohibit advertising, promotion, and sponsorship (S.14(c))
Monitor compliance with the Act and take enforcement action (S.14(d))
Conduct and facilitate research on tobacco and alcohol use and its effects (S.14(e))
Promote public awareness and education campaigns (S.14(f))
Coordinate with international organizations including the WHO (S.14(g))
Designate Authorized Officers for enforcement (S.16)
Appoint, dismiss, and exercise disciplinary control over employees (S.21)
Acquire, hold, and dispose of property (S.15)
Maintain a Fund for the Authority (S.22)
Enter into contracts and do all things necessary for carrying out its functions (S.15)
Make regulations with ministerial approval under S.30

dissentProtected.source: Majority vote; Chairman has casting vote (S.12)

chairType.source: Chairman appointed by the President from among appointed members (S.8)
chairType.label: Appointed

compositionDiversity.source: Chairman appointed by the President from among appointed members (S.8) + member mix

powers: Provide pre-hospital care ambulance services and emergency response services free of charge (S.3)
Provide immediate and effective pre-hospital care in a safe clinical environment until a person reaches the nearest healthcare provider (S.3)
Acquire, hold, and dispose of movable and immovable property (S.4-5)
Enter into contracts and agreements for the discharge of its functions (S.4)
Open and maintain bank accounts (S.4)
Receive grants, gifts, and donations (S.4)
Appoint a Chief Executive Officer (S.16)
Employ staff as necessary for efficient discharge of functions (S.17)
Maintain the Fund of the Foundation (S.18)
Make rules with Ministerial approval and gazetting (S.25)

bodies.name: NMRA

meetingClarity.source: Not specified in Act — meetings called as needed by CEO (S.10)

reportingMandated.source: Annual report laid before Parliament (S.23); annual audit (S.20)

dissentProtected.source: Majority vote; Chairman has casting vote (S.10)

chairType.source: Chairman appointed by the Minister in consultation with the Authority (S.5)
chairType.label: Appointed

compositionDiversity.source: Chairman appointed by the Minister in consultation with the Authority (S.5) + member mix

powers: Ensure availability of efficacious and safe medicines at affordable prices (S.3)
Promote rational use of medicines (S.3)
Encourage local manufacturing of essential medicines (S.3)
Register and license medicines, medical devices, and borderline products (S.14)
Set Maximum Retail Prices (MRP) for medicines (S.14, S.118)
Regulate manufacture, importation, sale, and distribution of medicines (S.49)
Enforce Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) per WHO standards (S.51)
Conduct post-marketing surveillance and pharmacovigilance (S.14)
Regulate clinical trials (S.14)
License pharmacies and enforce Good Pharmacy Practices (S.119-120)
Ban or withdraw medicines, devices, or products from use (S.108)
Designate Authorized Officers for enforcement (S.124)
Appoint CEO and staff (S.15-16)
Maintain Fund of the Authority from regulatory fees (S.18)
Make rules and regulations with ministerial approval (S.141-142)

alerts.description: Health Council has mandated regular meetings but operational status is "unknown". The mandate exists on paper but compliance cannot be verified.

Poisons, Opium & Dangerous Drugs Ordinance has functional mandates in 3 areas but establishes no statutory bodies. Governance is exercised through existing institutions.
